column1: What does XOR represent in binary addition?
column2: The sum of two bits without carry — 1^1=0, 1^0=1, 0^0=0.

column1: How do you compute the carry in bitwise addition?
column2: (a & b) << 1 — AND finds where both bits are set, left shift moves the carry to the correct position.

column1: Why is a loop needed in the bitwise addition approach?
column2: XOR and AND+shift produce two new numbers that still need to be added; repeat until AND (carry) becomes 0.

column1: Time and space complexity of bitwise addition (no + operator)?
column2: O(1) time and space — bounded by fixed 32-bit integer width.

column1: What is the DP recurrence for Counting Bits?
column2: bits[i] = 1 + bits[i - p], where p is the largest power of 2 ≤ i.

column1: What pattern do sections between powers of 2 follow in bit counts?
column2: Each section [2^k, 2^(k+1)-1] mirrors the previous section with every value incremented by 1.

column1: Time and space complexity of the DP approach for Counting Bits?
When should you look for power-of-2 boundaries instead of constant intervals?
column2: When counting or comparing bit properties — bit patterns reset and mirror at powers of 2, not at constant steps.
